name: storage_year_simulation
_purpose: NFR-6 — storage longevity across a full academic year (36 weeks) at national medium adoption.
_dataset_notes: Full national medium-adoption sizing: 280 teachers × 4 classrooms × 25
students = 28,000 students (≈27,900 per the capacity model adoption ×
weeks/hot_weeks are 0 because storage_walk mode short-circuits the
classrooms/students are created up-front; runs are appended week by
week by the storage walk loop).
_nfr: Closes NFR-6 — storage longevity. Pass: final pg_database_size < 10 GB after 36 weeks at full national medium adoption.
_traffic_notes: No k6 traffic. NFR-6 measures the cumulative storage trajectory of the
weekly compaction job over a 36-week horizon, not HTTP latency. The
run_scenario.py storage_walk branch skips pre/post analytics and the
_verification_notes: total_weeks=36 simulates one full academic year (September → mid-June).
compact_through_week_index=35 mirrors production behaviour: week 36 is
the 'current hot week' and is never compacted until it rolls over. The
scenario seeds 36 weeks but compacts only 35.
min_host_free_bytes=4 GB: storage walk aborts with a clear error if host
free disk drops below 4 GB at any iteration (leaves room for OS, WAL,
max_pg_db_bytes=15 GB: storage walk aborts if pg_database_size exceeds
15 GB at any iteration. This is HIGHER than the NFR-6 target of 10 GB —
it's a hard-stop ceiling, not the pass/fail criterion. The 10 GB target
Expected runtime: ~2–3 hours (seeding dominates; one week ≈ 4–6 min for
28K students × 5 runs × 20 turns = 2.8M turn events per week).
If aborted by max_pg_db_bytes, that IS a thesis finding: the year-horizon
budget on this VPS would be breached at week N.
